# RCA — the dock talked to its own echo for four turns (2026-07-23)

**Status:** root cause confirmed, **not fixed**. No reliable signal exists at the
decision point; the options all trade something real. Decision pending.

**Impact:** four LLM turns, ~45s, zero human speech. The dock answered garbled
transcripts of its own voice, and the chain only broke when a listening window
happened to time out.

## What happened

Reported as: *"turn-b90ae163 — no response from me, it ended the session and its
followups started a new session, none of which I spoke, all echo."*

Timeline from `conv_events` (dock-redmi, session s-moh5, all times IST):

| time | event | note |
|---|---|---|
| 12:43:20 | `mood:curious.wonder` self-thought turn starts | idle raiser, nobody spoke |
| 12:43:26 | `tts-start` | dock says *"Hmm, I just noticed some movement around, and Shwetha was here a moment ago looking a bit angry. Everything alright with you, Guru?"* |
| 12:43:28 | `vad:onset` → **`barge:skip:self-motion`** | its own voice; pause suppressed because the body was gesturing |
| 12:43:34 | `stt:final` tier **good** → `queue:busy` | *"I just keep the movement around and put your own container."* — mangled echo of the line above |
| 12:43:35 | `tts-end`, then **`drain:ran`** | the queued echo becomes a turn |
| 12:43:35 | `conv:idle reconnect` | phone reconnects mid-loop → **new session** |
| 12:43:43 | `vad:onset` → `barge:skip:self-motion` | again |
| 12:43:47 | `stt:final` tier shaky → **`RAN-TURN`** via `followup-window` | *"If you're coming by the curve here."* |
| 12:43:54 | `vad:onset` → `barge:skip:self-motion` | again |
| 12:43:57 | `stt:final` tier shaky → **`RAN-TURN`** via `followup-window` | *"You can have me by the room of the train."* |
| 12:44:03 | `vad:onset` → `barge:skip:self-motion` | again |
| 12:44:14 | `conv:idle window-timeout` | loop ends only because the window expired |

## Root cause

The dock's own TTS returns through the mic (phone AEC leaves residue;
`STT_ECHO_GATE` is deliberately OFF so barge-in can hear the user). Each reply
therefore produces an utterance, and **every gate that could have stopped it
passed**:

- **Barge pause** — would have muted the pressure, but `barge:skip:self-motion`
  suppressed it on all four onsets (the 1.8s motion mute re-arms with every
  gesture, and the dock gestures throughout a reply). Same defect measured this
  morning at **71% false-suppression** (`probe.mjs suppressed-barges`).
- **Voiced-fraction gate** — the echoes committed just AFTER `tts-end`, so they
  met the 10% idle floor, not the 35% speaking floor. One at 17% was dropped
  while speaking; the ones that ran arrived a beat later.
- **Confidence tier** — the first echo was rated **good** by parakeet.
- **Admission** — entrance A was the **busy queue** (heard while `speaking` →
  queued → `drain:ran`); entrance B was the **followup window** (8s after each
  reply, which every echo landed inside).
- **Prompt framing** — the possibly-overheard framing did not make the model
  stay silent.

## What does NOT work (measured, so nobody retries it)

- **Text similarity to what the dock just said.** Only the first echo resembles
  the source; AEC mangles the rest beyond recognition:

  | similarity | word overlap | echo text |
  |---|---|---|
  | 0.40 | 5 | "I just keep the movement around and put your own container." |
  | 0.18 | 1 | "If you're coming by the curve here." |
  | 0.06 | 1 | "You can have me by the room of the train." |

- **Confidence tiers** — echo scored `good`.
- **Voice fingerprint** — the obvious "is this the dock's own voice" check, but
  live scores today sit at 0.53-0.66 against a 0.5-ish bar; not separable yet
  (see [[local-speaker-id-diarization-test]]).

## Options (none free)

Barge-in has two halves, and they are affected differently:
**(i) the interrupt** — you talk, the dock stops (onset → pause → stop-intent,
fires BEFORE queueing); **(ii) content barge** — you say something substantive
over the reply and it is answered afterwards (busy-queue → drain).

1. **Status quo.** Loops stay possible. They are rare but self-amplifying and
   burn turns; the model also starts reacting to its own garbled claims (here:
   "Shwetha was angry" re-entering as fact).
2. **Reject any utterance overlapping a TTS window** (both entrances), with
   stop-intent exempted. Kills the loop structurally with a signal we already
   have exactly (`audioStartAt/audioEndAt` vs the speech window, one clock).
   **Cost: removes half (ii) — content barge-in.** Its practical value today is
   low (traces show mostly garbage: "Wait, start by M to Rickson", "Eight,
   seven, eighty, six") but that is partly BECAUSE echo pollutes it — circular,
   and worth stating.
3. **Cancel the echo at the source** (the real fix, "Fix B" of
   [2026-07-21-barge-stop-continues.md](2026-07-21-barge-stop-continues.md)):
   the station SYNTHESISES the TTS, so it holds the reference signal — a
   station-side AEC / spectral subtraction against known-played audio, or
   stronger device AEC. Preserves both halves. Real engineering, not a
   threshold.

**Recommendation:** 2 as a stopgap, 3 as the answer. Not implemented — both
change conversational feel, so the call is the user's.

## Also surfaced

`conv:idle reconnect` at 12:43:35 (phone re-connect mid-loop) is what split the
session. Whether that was a WS blip or a consequence of the rapid turn churn is
unexamined.

## Reproduce / verify

```bash
# the incident, on one timeline
curl "localhost:8099/api/observability/incident?dock=dock-redmi&from=1784790780000&to=1784790860000&format=md"
# suppressed barges that had real speech behind them
node docs/monitoring/probe.mjs suppressed-barges --hours 24
```
Clips are kept: `GET /api/perception/utterance-audio/dock-redmi/<audioStartMs>`.
